# Classification Rotation Forest Learner

Classification using Rotation Forest algorithm. Training this learner
works only for tasks with at least two features. The task must not have
utf8 feature names. Calls
[`rotationForest::rotationForest()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/rotationForest/man/rotationForest.html)
from
[rotationForest](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=rotationForest).

## Dictionary

This [Learner](https://mlr3.mlr-org.com/reference/Learner.html) can be
instantiated via
[lrn()](https://mlr3.mlr-org.com/reference/mlr_sugar.html):

    lrn("classif.rotationForest")

## Meta Information

- Task type: “classif”

- Predict Types: “response”, “prob”

- Feature Types: “integer”, “numeric”

- Required Packages: [mlr3](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=mlr3),
  [mlr3extralearners](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=mlr3extralearners),
  [rotationForest](https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=rotationForest)

## Parameters

|     |         |         |                  |
|-----|---------|---------|------------------|
| Id  | Type    | Default | Range            |
| K   | integer | 3       | \\\[1, \infty)\\ |
| L   | integer | 10      | \\\[1, \infty)\\ |

## References

Rodriguez, J.J., Kuncheva, L.I., Alonso, C.J. (2006). “Rotation Forest:
A New Classifier Ensemble Method.” *IEEE Transactions on Pattern
Analysis and Machine Intelligence*, **28**(10), 1619-1630.
[doi:10.1109/TPAMI.2006.211](https://doi.org/10.1109/TPAMI.2006.211) .

### `LearnerClassifRotationForest$new()`

Creates a new instance of this
[R6](https://r6.r-lib.org/reference/R6Class.html) class.

#### Usage

    LearnerClassifRotationForest$new()

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### `LearnerClassifRotationForest$clone()`

The objects of this class are cloneable with this method.

#### Usage

    LearnerClassifRotationForest$clone(deep = FALSE)

#### Arguments

- `deep`:

  Whether to make a deep clone.

## Examples

``` r
# Define the Learner
learner = lrn("classif.rotationForest")
print(learner)
#> 
#> ── <LearnerClassifRotationForest> (classif.rotationForest): Rotation Forest ────
#> • Model: -
#> • Parameters: list()
#> • Packages: mlr3, mlr3extralearners, and rotationForest
#> • Predict Types: [response] and prob
#> • Feature Types: integer and numeric
#> • Encapsulation: none (fallback: -)
#> • Properties: twoclass
#> • Other settings: use_weights = 'error', predict_raw = 'FALSE'

# Define a Task
task = tsk("sonar")

# Create train and test set
ids = partition(task)

# Train the learner on the training ids
learner$train(task, row_ids = ids$train)

print(learner$model)
```
